item.title: Founder of Fallen Chinese Property Giant Evergrande Pleads Guilty to Fraud
item.description: Hui Ka Yan, once one of the world’s richest men, was accused along with the developer whose implosion helped spark a property crisis in China.

item.title: Opinion | To Beat China, Embrace Open-Source AI
item.description: Proprietary systems were less risky, U.S. officials assumed. That turned out to be a dangerous premise.

item.title: Pull of the American Dream Weakens for China’s Best and Brightest
item.description: U.S. immigration hurdles and fear of crime, amplified by state media, have become reasons for Chinese talent to stay home.

item.title: China’s Economy Starts Year on Strong Footing, but Iran Risks Loom
item.description: Beijing reported first-quarter GDP growth of 5%, driven by robust exports, but shock waves from the Iran war threaten its momentum.

item.title: What the U.S.-Iran Conflict Reveals About China’s Diplomacy
item.description: Analyst Drew Thompson explains how China’s restrained public response to Trump’s Iran campaign reflects a deliberate diplomatic approach by Beijing.

item.title: China Sharpens Retaliatory Tools Against U.S. Ahead of Trump Summit
item.description: Beijing has responded to Washington’s trade pressure with broad but vague threats aimed at foreign businesses.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
